daily_reality.typical_day: Starts 7:30. Checks tender portals, construction news, LinkedIn for new opportunities. 50% of business is public tenders, 50% GU-Anfragen (subcontractor inquiries from general contractors). Manages relationships with 20 Generalunternehmer. Attends 2-3 Vergabeverhandlungen per month.
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Splitting time between portal monitoring and relationship selling. No unified view of public + private opportunity pipeline. Double-entering data between portals and Salesforce.
daily_reality.weekly_volume: Monitors ~120 tenders/week across portals, identifies ~15 relevant, pursues ~5 (mix of public and GU inquiries)
decision_criteria: Technical specifications match capabilities (no Structural glazing if not certified)

psychology.tech_savviness: high — uses CRM, analytics, and digital sales tools daily
psychology.risk_tolerance: moderate — willing to bid on stretch projects if the GU relationship is strong
psychology.decision_speed: fast — needs to respond quickly to GU inquiries, slower for public tenders
trust_signals: professional UI suitable for client meetings
frustration_triggers: can't combine public and private tender pipeline in one view
notifications that are too late (after Submissionsfrist)
tool looks unprofessional when sharing screen with GU clients
delight_triggers: mobile app for on-the-go pipeline checks

first_screen: Unified pipeline: public tenders + private inquiries in one view
Filter by stage (new, evaluating, bidding, awarded, lost)
Key metrics: pipeline value, win rate, response time
must_have: CRM integration or at least CSV export for Salesforce
Team collaboration (share tenders, assign to colleagues)
Professional appearance (will show to GU partners)
nice_to_have: GU partner scoring (which GUs give us the most work)
expectations.current_pain_level: 7/10 — managing two separate pipelines (public/private) is unsustainable as company grows
